Innovating for customers and our industry

We’re transforming how we design, build and operate to improve aerospace efficiency and performance.

Investing in efficiency for customers, industry and our operations

Aerospace protects and connects people, enables economic growth and trade, provides humanitarian relief and allows for human exploration of space.
Boeing collaborates globally with customers and stakeholders to support responsible growth for our industry, and we invest in innovation that improves the efficiency and sustainability of air travel and our operations.

Aviation Efficiency

Boeing is designing and building more efficient airplanes for our customers

Fleet renewal supports the aviation industry’s long-term growth and sustainability goals. Our commercial airplane family reduces fuel use and CO2 emissions by 20-30% and significantly reduces noise for local communities.

Performance

20% Less fuel
20% Less CO2
50% Smaller noise footprint
Compared to original Next-Generation 737

Key Technology

737 MAX Advanced Technology winglets
737 MAX AT winglets increase lift and effective wingspan while reducing drag, improving fuel efficiency up to 2%

Performance

25% Less fuel
25% Less CO2
50% Smaller noise footprint
Compared to 767-300ER

Key Technology

Carbon-composite fuselage and wings
The 787’s strong, lighter-weight structure and advanced aerodynamics optimize performance and reduce fuel use

Performance

20% Less fuel
20% Less CO2
40% Smaller noise footprint
Compared to 777-300ER

Key Technology

High-span composite wing
A generational leap in aerodynamic technology

Performance

30% Less fuel
30% Less CO2
60% Smaller noise footprint

Compared to 747-400F

Key Technology

GE9X engines
The largest, most powerful and most fuel-efficient engine in its class, designed for the 777X family

Boeing ecoDemonstrator program

Our flight demonstrators test and validate new technologies that improve safety and efficiency in airplanes and fleet operations

meet the ecodemonstrator

Global Collaboration

Sustainable Aviation Fuels (SAF)

Boeing has led the aviation industry in making SAF a reality, and we collaborate globally with airlines, governments, industry and research institutions to increase SAF supply and reduce its price. SAF – made from used cooking oil, non-edible plants, agricultural waste, industrial plant off-gassing and other sustainable feedstocks – can lower lifecycle CO2 emissions by up to 80% when it's neat, or unblended. Today, commercial aircraft are certified to fly on blends of up to 50% SAF with conventional jet fuel.
SAF fact sheet

Leveraging data to inform aviation’s future

The Boeing Cascade Climate Impact Model explores ways to improve efficiencies and reduce emissions as our industry grows.

Explore Cascade

Sustainable Operations

Building more efficient products in a sustainable way

Boeing understands that sustainability starts within our own operations. That’s why we set targets and measure performance, recognize sustainable practices, invest in innovative projects at our sites and reduce resource use.
 
Boeing has four strategies to advance sustainable operations: Innovation and Engagement, Efficiency and Conservation, Site Infrastructure and Investment, and Resilience and Risk Management.
Operations Fact Sheet

Efficiency and Conservation

Responsible aerospace begins inside our facilities because efficient operations cut costs, conserve resources and prevent pollution. We work to optimize building and equipment performance and utilize SAF in our operations.

Site Infrastructure and Investment

We invest strategically in our site infrastructure to drive efficiency and reduce cost. And we prioritize LEED standards and implement renewable energy plans in support of our goals.

Resilience and Risk Management

Boeing is committed to restoring legacy industrial sites to support economic and environmental well-being of local communities. We recognize our responsibility for past practices and work as a long-term partner to help repair damage. We coordinate with local governments and communities to make tangible progress for people, wildlife, and the environment.

Duwamish Waterway
Santa Susana Restoration
